messages.subject: [OGRUG] Software Carpentry
messages.body_plain:
I'd be interested in the Software Carpentry instructor training. Not sure if I can offer the group a space yet, but I"d be interested in giving a seminar at Health Canada in the next few months. 

Daniel Buijs
dbuijs at gmail.com


On 2015-10-29, at 7:00 AM, r-ug-ottawa-request at r-project.org wrote:

> Send R-UG-Ottawa mailing list submissions to
> 	r-ug-ottawa at r-project.org
> 
> To subscribe or unsubscribe via the World Wide Web, visit
> 	https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-ug-ottawa
> or, via email, send a message with subject or body 'help' to
> 	r-ug-ottawa-request at r-project.org
> 
> You can reach the person managing the list at
> 	r-ug-ottawa-owner at r-project.org
> 
> When replying, please edit your Subject line so it is more specific
> than "Re: Contents of R-UG-Ottawa digest..."
> 
> 
> Today's Topics:
> 
>   1. Re: R-UG-Ottawa Digest, Vol 38, Issue 1 (ProfJCNash)
>   2. Re: R-UG-Ottawa Digest, Vol 38, Issue 1 (Chris Cole)
>   3. Re: R-UG-Ottawa Digest, Vol 38, Issue 1 (Tyler Smith)
> 
> 
> ----------------------------------------------------------------------
> 
> Message: 1
> Date: Wed, 28 Oct 2015 08:37:57 -0400
> From: ProfJCNash <profjcnash at gmail.com>
> To: r-ug-ottawa at r-project.org
> Subject: Re: [OGRUG] R-UG-Ottawa Digest, Vol 38, Issue 1
> Message-ID: <5630C1A5.4060201 at gmail.com>
> Content-Type: text/plain; charset=windows-1252; format=flowed
> 
> uottawa is probably "easy" on a Saturday, less so (doors locked) on 
> Sunday, and essentially impossible to book rooms other times due to 
> regular needs.
> 
> When are the dates envisaged?
> 
> JN
> 
> 
> On 15-10-28 07:00 AM, r-ug-ottawa-request at r-project.org wrote:
>> Send R-UG-Ottawa mailing list submissions to
>> 	r-ug-ottawa at r-project.org
>> 
>> To subscribe or unsubscribe via the World Wide Web, visit
>> 	https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-ug-ottawa
>> or, via email, send a message with subject or body 'help' to
>> 	r-ug-ottawa-request at r-project.org
>> 
>> You can reach the person managing the list at
>> 	r-ug-ottawa-owner at r-project.org
>> 
>> When replying, please edit your Subject line so it is more specific
>> than "Re: Contents of R-UG-Ottawa digest..."
>> 
>> 
>> Today's Topics:
>> 
>>    1. software carpentry training in December (Tyler Smith)
>>    2. Re: software carpentry training in December (Alex Demarsh)
>> 
>> 
>> ----------------------------------------------------------------------
>> 
>> Message: 1
>> Date: Tue, 27 Oct 2015 10:07:35 -0400
>> From: Tyler Smith <tyler at plantarum.ca>
>> To: r-ug-ottawa at r-project.org
>> Subject: [OGRUG] software carpentry training in December
>> Message-ID:
>> 	<1445954855.2824201.421510401.751BF985 at webmail.messagingengine.com>
>> Content-Type: text/plain
>> 
>> Hi folks,
>> 
>> The Software Carpentry group has announced a new round of R instructor
>> training. This is a two-day workshop this December, aimed at proficient
>> R users who wish to learn to train others to use R. They also work with
>> Bash, Python, Git, SQL, and other tools useful in data analysis. The
>> details are here:
>> 
>> http://software-carpentry.org/blog/2015/10/call-for-instructor-training.html
>> 
>> The training is free, but there is an expectation that instructors will
>> host a 2-day training workshop for new R users at their own institution
>> in the 6 months following the workshop. The training will actually be
>> delivered via web conference, so we can 'attend' wherever we like, given
>> we can get 6-8 people together with a reliable web connection.
>> 
>> Questions for the list:
>> 
>> 1. Is anyone interested (or know someone who might be) in joining me on
>> an application to attend the instructor training?
>> 2. If so, are you interested in hosting an R training course sometime
>> next spring? I'm happy to coordinate this, or help to do so, however is
>> useful. I can host here at Ag Canada (Carling Avenue), but another venue
>> would be preferable as we have really spotty internet connection in our
>> conference rooms.
>> 
>> Best,
>> 
>> Tyler
>> 
> 
> 
> 
> ------------------------------
> 
> Message: 2
> Date: Wed, 28 Oct 2015 09:44:42 -0400
> From: Chris Cole <chris.c.1221 at gmail.com>
> To: ProfJCNash <profjcnash at gmail.com>
> Cc: r-ug-ottawa at r-project.org
> Subject: Re: [OGRUG] R-UG-Ottawa Digest, Vol 38, Issue 1
> Message-ID:
> 	<CAFut0+_kD0Mb8e-h-kmyJ9ncx6WQL29EAjzK_6fcS12Q26767w at mail.gmail.com>
> Content-Type: text/plain; charset="UTF-8"
> 
> I would be interested in joining the instructor training, though I can't
> guarantee teaching a course in the next 6 months.
> 
> Chris
> 
> On Wed, Oct 28, 2015 at 8:37 AM, ProfJCNash <profjcnash at gmail.com> wrote:
> 
>> uottawa is probably "easy" on a Saturday, less so (doors locked) on
>> Sunday, and essentially impossible to book rooms other times due to regular
>> needs.
>> 
>> When are the dates envisaged?
>> 
>> JN
>> 
>> 
>> On 15-10-28 07:00 AM, r-ug-ottawa-request at r-project.org wrote:
>>

>>> Hi folks,
>>> 
>>> The Software Carpentry group has announced a new round of R instructor
>>> training. This is a two-day workshop this December, aimed at proficient
>>> R users who wish to learn to train others to use R. They also work with
>>> Bash, Python, Git, SQL, and other tools useful in data analysis. The
>>> details are here:
>>> 
>>> 
>>> http://software-carpentry.org/blog/2015/10/call-for-instructor-training.html
>>> 
>>> The training is free, but there is an expectation that instructors will
>>> host a 2-day training workshop for new R users at their own institution
>>> in the 6 months following the workshop. The training will actually be
>>> delivered via web conference, so we can 'attend' wherever we like, given
>>> we can get 6-8 people together with a reliable web connection.
>>> 
>>> Questions for the list:
>>> 
>>> 1. Is anyone interested (or know someone who might be) in joining me on
>>> an application to attend the instructor training?
>>> 2. If so, are you interested in hosting an R training course sometime
>>> next spring? I'm happy to coordinate this, or help to do so, however is
>>> useful. I can host here at Ag Canada (Carling Avenue), but another venue
>>> would be preferable as we have really spotty internet connection in our
>>> conference rooms.
>>> 
>>> Best,
>>> 
>>> Tyler
>>> 
>>> 
>> _______________________________________________
>> R-UG-Ottawa mailing list
>> R-UG-Ottawa at r-project.org
>> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-ug-ottawa
>>

> Message: 3
> Date: Wed, 28 Oct 2015 09:58:31 -0400
> From: Tyler Smith <tyler at plantarum.ca>
> To: r-ug-ottawa at r-project.org
> Subject: Re: [OGRUG] R-UG-Ottawa Digest, Vol 38, Issue 1
> Message-ID:

> The dates are already set, December 7 and 8 (Monday and Tuesday).
> 
> Best,
> 
> Tyler
>

messages.body_snippet:
I'd be interested in the Software Carpentry instructor training. Not sure if I can offer the group a space yet, but I"d be interested in giving a seminar at Health Canada in the next few months. 

Dan
